testcase.name: compile/uint_fold.lean
testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./uint_fold.lean.out 12760 12720 11 6 44 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run uint_fold.lean 12760 12720 11 6 44

testcase.name: compile/unreachable.lean
testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./unreachable.lean.out Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run unreachable.lean

testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./uset.lean.out 1 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run uset.lean 1

testcase.name: compile/wait_dedicated.lean
testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./wait_dedicated.lean.out Hi there Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run wait_dedicated.lean Hi there

testcase.name: compile_bench/binarytrees.lean
testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./binarytrees.lean.out 14 stretch tree of depth 15 check: 65535 16384 trees of depth 4 check: 507904 4096 trees of depth 6 check: 520192 1024 trees of depth 8 check: 523264 256 trees of depth 10 check: 524032 64 trees of depth 12 check: 524224 16 trees of depth 14 check: 524272 long lived tree of depth 14 check: 32767 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run binarytrees.lean 14 stretch tree of depth 15 check: 65535 16384 trees of depth 4 check: 507904 4096 trees of depth 6 check: 520192 1024 trees of depth 8 check: 523264 256 trees of depth 10 check: 524032 64 trees of depth 12 check: 524224 16 trees of depth 14 check: 524272 long lived tree of depth 14 check: 32767

testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./binarytrees.st.lean.out 14 stretch tree of depth 15 check: 65535 16384 trees of depth 4 check: 507904 4096 trees of depth 6 check: 520192 1024 trees of depth 8 check: 523264 256 trees of depth 10 check: 524032 64 trees of depth 12 check: 524224 16 trees of depth 14 check: 524272 long lived tree of depth 14 check: 32767 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run binarytrees.st.lean 14 stretch tree of depth 15 check: 65535 16384 trees of depth 4 check: 507904 4096 trees of depth 6 check: 520192 1024 trees of depth 8 check: 523264 256 trees of depth 10 check: 524032 64 trees of depth 12 check: 524224 16 trees of depth 14 check: 524272 long lived tree of depth 14 check: 32767

[This part of the test output was removed since it exceeds the threshold of 1024 bytes.]

testcase.name: compile_bench/const_fold.lean
testcase.system-out:
Setting stack size to unlimited Compiling and executing lean file const_fold.lean:55:2: warning: unused variable `e`
Note: This linter can be disabled with `set_option linter.unusedVariables false` const_fold.lean:64:6: warning: unused variable `x`
Note: This linter can be disabled with `set_option linter.unusedVariables false` ++ ./const_fold.lean.out 15 93011 93011 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run const_fold.lean 15 93011 93011

Compiling and executing lean file deriv.lean:13:2: warning: unused variable `a`
Note: This linter can be disabled with `set_option linter.unusedVariables false` ++ ./deriv.lean.out 7 1 count: 6 2 count: 22 3 count: 90 4 count: 420 5 count: 2202 6 count: 12886 7 count: 83648 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run deriv.lean 7 1 count: 6 2 count: 22 3 count: 90 4 count: 420 5 count: 2202 6 count: 12886 7 count: 83648

testcase.name: compile_bench/hashmap.lean
testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./hashmap.lean.out 11 100 measurement: containsHit 11.726700 s measurement: containsMiss 25.098700 s measurement: iterate 72.115500 s measurement: insertIfNewHit 13.779100 s measurement: insertHit 25.976100 s measurement: insertMissEmpty 55.033800 s measurement: insertMissEmptyWithCapacity 32.566500 s measurement: eraseInsert 68.931300 s Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run hashmap.lean 11 100 measurement: containsHit 1496.246400 s measurement: containsMiss 2160.990300 s measurement: iterate 1253.203200 s measurement: insertIfNewHit 1592.837800 s measurement: insertHit 2224.614000 s measurement: insertMissEmpty 4845.880000 s measurement: insertMissEmptyWithCapacity 2080.380000 s measurement: eraseInsert 5419.747700 s

testcase.system-out: Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run identifier_completion.lean measurement: completion 2.339000 s

testcase.name: compile_bench/ilean_roundtrip.lean
testcase.system-out: Compiling and executing lean file ++ ./ilean_roundtrip.lean.out 1000 measurement: compress 0.008000 s measurement: parse 0.005000 s Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run ilean_roundtrip.lean 1000 measurement: compress 0.004000 s measurement: parse 0.002000 s

testcase.name: compile_bench/iterators.lean
testcase.system-out:
Compiling and executing lean file iterators.lean:55:5: warning: `Std.Iter.count` has been deprecated: Use `Std.Iter.length` instead
Note: The updated constant has a different type: {α β : Type w} → [inst : Std.Iterator α Id β] → [Std.IteratorLoop α Id Id] → Std.Iter β → Nat instead of {α β : Type u_1} → [inst : Std.Iterator α Id β] → [Std.IteratorLoop α Id Id] → Std.Iter β → Nat ++ ./iterators.lean.out xs[0] = 0 xs[10000] = 10000 xs[20000] = 20000 xs[30000] = 30000 xs[40000] = 40000 xs[50000] = 50000 xs[60000] = 60000 xs[70000] = 70000 xs[80000] = 80000 xs[90000] = 90000 xs[0] = 0 xs[10000] = 10000 xs[20000] = 20000 xs[30000] = 30000 xs[40000] = 40000 xs[50000] = 50000 xs[60000] = 60000 xs[70000] = 70000 xs[80000] = 80000 xs[90000] = 90000 Interpreting lean file ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false -j1 --run iterators.lean xs[0] = 0 xs[10000] = 10000 xs[20000] = 20000 xs[30000] = 30000 xs[40000] = 40000 xs[50000] = 50000 xs[60000] = 60000 xs[70000] = 70000 xs[80000] = 80000 xs[90000] = ... [This part of the test output was removed since it exceeds the threshold of 1024 bytes.]

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0001.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0001.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.anon (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_ident `x)) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0002.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0002.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named_no_paren `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "1"))) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0003.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0003.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named "(" `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "1")) ")") All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0004.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0004.txt Failure @0 (⟨1, 0⟩): expected '(', '+', '-', expected identifier, string, or number or token Final stack: <missing> Remaining: "\n(x:=1)"

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0005.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0005.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.flag_on "+" `foo) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0006.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0006.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.flag_off "-" `other) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0007.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0007.txt Failure @2 (⟨1, 2⟩): expected no space before Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.flag_off "-" `other) Remaining: "other"

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0008.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0008.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named "(" `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "1")) ")") Remaining: "\n"

testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0009.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named_no_paren `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_ident `y)) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0010.txt

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0011.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0011.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named_no_paren `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_str (str "\"y\""))) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0012.txt
testcase.system-out:
++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0012.txt Failure @3 (⟨1, 3⟩): unterminated string literal Final stack: • `x • ":=" • (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_str <missing>)
Remaining: "\"y"

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0013.txt
testcase.system-out:
++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0013.txt 2 failures: @7 (⟨1, 7⟩): expected ')' "" @7 (⟨1, 7⟩): unterminated string literal ""
Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named "(" `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_str <missing>) <missing>)

testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0014.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.anon (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "42"))) All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0015.txt
testcase.system-out:
++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0015.txt 4 failures: @4 (⟨1, 4⟩): expected ')' "" @4 (⟨1, 4⟩): expected ':=' "" @4 (⟨1, 4⟩): expected token "" @4 (⟨1, 4⟩): unexpected end of input ""
Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named "(" <missing> <missing> <missing> <missing>)

testcase.name: docparse/arg_0016.txt
testcase.system-out:
++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0016.txt 3 failures: @6 (⟨1, 6⟩): expected ')' "" @6 (⟨1, 6⟩): expected ':=' "" @6 (⟨1, 6⟩): unexpected end of input ""
Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named "(" `x <missing> <missing> <missing>)

testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean arg_0017.txt Failure @8 (⟨1, 8⟩): expected ')' Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.named "(" `x ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "42")) <missing>) Remaining: "\n)"

testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean args_0001.txt Success! Final stack: [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.anon (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "43")))] Remaining: "\n\"foo\""

testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean args_0003.txt Success! Final stack: [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.named_no_paren `dialect ":=" (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_str (str "\"chicken\""))) (Lean.Doc.Syntax.anon (Lean.Doc.Syntax.arg_num (num "43")))] Remaining: "\n(foo)"

testcase.name: docparse/block_0001.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean block_0001.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.ul "ul{" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.li "*" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.para "para{" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.text (str "\"Here's a bullet\""))] "}")]) (Lean.Doc.Syntax.li "*" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.para "para{" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.text (str "\"and another with some code in it\""))] "}") (Lean.Doc.Syntax.codeblock "````" [`lean []] "\n" (str "\"hey\\n\\nthere\\n\"") "````")]) (Lean.Doc.Syntax.li "*" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.para "para{" [(Lean.Doc.Syntax.text (str "\"and another one\"")) (Lean.Doc.Syntax.linebreak "line!" (str "\"\\n\""))] "}")])] "}") All input consumed.

testcase.name: docparse/block_0002.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean block_0002.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.command "{" `test [] "}") Remaining: "Here's a paragraph."

testcase.name: docparse/block_0003.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean block_0003.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.command "{" `test [] "}") Remaining: " Here's a paragraph."

testcase.name: docparse/block_0004.txt
testcase.system-out: ++ lean -Dlinter.all=false --run run_test.lean block_0004.txt Success! Final stack: (Lean.Doc.Syntax.command "{" `test [] "}") Remaining: " Here's a paragraph."
